name: Import Gerber and center geometry
description: Tests that the agent correctly imports a Gerber file, extracts shapes using parentheses indexing, and centers the geometry using .Vertices
prompt: I have a Gerber file at 'GERBER_PATH'. Import it into MATLAB, extract the metal layer shape, center it at the origin, and display it.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses gerberRead to import the Gerber file
Uses parentheses indexing shp(1) not brace indexing shp{1} to access the polygon array
assertions.expected: shp(1) or equivalent parentheses indexing on the shapes result

assertions.description: Uses .Vertices property to get coordinates for centering, not mesh()
Does not use mesh() on bare antenna.Polygon which errors without MaxEdgeLength

name: Inspect a native PCB board file
description: Tests that the agent can open a native board and navigate the hierarchy: cadnets, components, layers
prompt: I have a PCB board in native format at 'BOARD_PATH'. Open it and tell me how many layers, cadnets, and components it has. Then list the first 5 cadnets and the first 5 components.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses pcbFileRead to open the native board
Uses cadnetList to enumerate nets
Uses componentList to enumerate components
Reports NumLayers, NumCadnets, and NumComponents from the pcbFileRead object
assertions.expected: NumLayers or NumCadnets or NumComponents property access

name: Gerber to pcbComponent workflow
description: Tests the full import pipeline: stackUp, PCBReader, pcbComponent conversion
prompt: I have a Gerber file at 'GERBER_PATH' representing a top copper layer. Set up a stackup with this as the top metal on a 1.6mm FR4 substrate with a solid ground plane, import it as a pcbComponent, and show the result.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Creates a stackUp object
Creates a PCBReader with the stackUp
Converts to pcbComponent
Layer1 is air/thickness, Layer2 is the Gerber file — follows the stackUp numbering convention
assertions.expected: Layer1 assigned a thickness, Layer2 assigned the Gerber filename

name: Import Allegro .brd with extracta check
description: Tests that the agent handles the extractaSetup prerequisite when importing Cadence Allegro .brd files
prompt: I have a Cadence Allegro board file at 'BRD_PATH'. Import it into MATLAB so I can inspect its layers and nets.

assertions.description: Mentions or calls extractaSetup() before attempting pcbFileRead on the .brd file
assertions.expected: extractaSetup referenced or called before pcbFileRead

assertions.description: Uses pcbFileRead to import the .brd file
Warns the user or checks that extracta is configured, since it requires a Cadence installation
assertions.expected: Explanation that extractaSetup is a prerequisite or prompt asking user to confirm Cadence is installed

assertions.description: Does not attempt pcbFileRead on a .brd without addressing the extracta dependency
assertions.expected: Calling pcbFileRead('...brd') without any mention of extractaSetup

name: Query imported board stackup as a table
description: Tests that the agent uses stackUp(pfile) to get the material table, not the stackUp constructor
prompt: I have a PCB board in native format at 'BOARD_PATH'. Import it and show me the full layer stackup with material properties. Also show just the top 3 layers.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Calls stackUp(pfile) as an object function on the pcbFileRead result, not the standalone stackUp constructor
assertions.expected: stackUp(pfile) or stackUp(pfile, ...) where pfile is the pcbFileRead object

assertions.description: Treats the result as a table (display, index rows, access columns), not as a stackUp object with Layer properties
assertions.expected: Table operations like disp(su), su(1:3,:), or su.LayerName — not su.Layer1

assertions.description: Does not create a standalone stackUp constructor to inspect the imported board
assertions.expected: S = stackUp; S.Layer1 or similar constructor usage for an imported board

name: Navigate part and component hierarchy correctly
description: Tests that the agent uses componentData on part objects, componentPinData on component objects, and metal-only indices for layer()
prompt: I have a PCB board in native format at 'BOARD_PATH'. Import it. Find the first part type, then list which components use that part. Also pick the first component and show its pin data. Finally, inspect the first metal layer of the board.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Calls componentData on a part object, not on a component object
assertions.expected: componentData(p) where p = part(pfile, ...) — not componentData(comp)

assertions.description: Uses componentPinData for pin information on a component
Uses pfile.MetalLayer to get valid layer indices for layer(), not hardcoded index 1
